subject: Project Management
title: The Theory of Constraints
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Theory of Constraints.

hook.text: In 1984, Eliyahu Goldratt wrote a novel about a factory manager. It became a management classic.

buildup.text: 'The Goal' taught that every system has exactly one constraint limiting its output.

discovery.text: Goldratt's five steps: identify, exploit, subordinate, elevate, repeat. Simple but powerful.

twist.text: Goldratt was a physicist, not a manager. He applied systems thinking to business.

climax.text: The Theory of Constraints gave Kanban its scientific foundation — flow has rules, and they work.

punchline.text: A physicist solved manufacturing with a novel.

quiz.question: Who created the Theory of Constraints?
